title: The Briar Ledger
summary: A dead archivist leaves behind a coded debt ledger and three missing names.

evidence.title: Coded Ledger Copy
evidence.summary: Three scratched-out entries sit beside initials tied to relief fund transfers.

evidence.title: Archive Clerk Correspondence
evidence.summary: Anxious archive messages describe which relief records were pulled before the archivist died.

evidence.title: Relief Disbursement Register
evidence.summary: A relief-fund register shows two payouts disappearing from the same accounting cycle.

stages.title: Ledger Decoding
stages.summary: Pull a usable code from the archivist's last ledger copy.
handlerPrompts: The repeating initials matter more than the scratched-out names.
Start with the relief cycle mark in the ledger margin.

objectives.prompt: Enter the repeating initials beside the erased entries.

stages.title: Relief Trail
stages.summary: Cross the decoded initials against the missing relief-fund records.
handlerPrompts: Two missing records disappear in the same relief cycle.
The clerk correspondence narrows which payouts the treasurer wanted buried.

objectives.prompt: Which two records disappeared in the same relief cycle?

stages.title: Final Attribution
stages.summary: Use the relief trail to identify who orchestrated the erasures.
handlerPrompts: The erased payments protect the person who signed the relief transfers.
Ask who profits if the archivist's scratched lines stay buried.
